title: Radank
content:

{{Quote|But it is unnatural. ''[…]'' the radank claw, as it grows, appears to be developing characteristics of a radank as it would appear farther up the leg, as if someone were slowly re-creating the entire creature through the absorption of your body. It is an odd pathology.|Shaper Nen Yim's analysis of Warmaster Tsavong Lah's radank claw graft|<ref name="Rebel Dream" />}}
'''Radanks''' were [[Sentience/Legends|non-sentient]] [[Diet/Legends|predators]] in existence by the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]], during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], when the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] invaded [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Radanks possessed clawed [[Limb/Legends|limbs]] and a proboscis, and Yuuzhan Vong [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] even replaced one of [[Gender/Legends|his]] arms with a grafted radank leg in a show of devotion to his [[Yun'o|gods]] during the war. In [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], after the war was over, at least one radank hunted in the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|undercity]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].

==Biology and appearance==
Beast-like, radanks featured a thin proboscis and both forward and hind [[Limb/Legends|limbs]]. The front legs of the radank<ref name="Tempest" /> were [[Color/Legends|red]] in coloration and were covered in scales and spines. Additionally, the beast's forelimbs had articulated digits that were segmented unnaturally compared to those of a [[Human/Legends|Human]] or a [[Yuuzhan Vong]],<ref name="Rebel Dream" /> with each digit topped with a claw<ref name="Destiny's Way">''[[The New Jedi Order: Destiny's Way]]''</ref> that could be sheathed in the finger;<ref name="Tempest" /> the digits of the claw resembled a pincer in some ways.<ref name="Rebel Stand">''[[The New Jedi Order: Enemy Lines II: Rebel Stand]]''</ref>

==Behavior==
[[Sentience/Legends|Non-sentient]] by classification, radanks were [[Diet/Legends|carnivorous]] in diet and occasionally hunted sentient beings. When startled, some radanks reared back on their hind legs and exposed their claws. Radanks also used their proboscises to sniff new prey.<ref name="Tempest" />

==Radanks in the galaxy==
[[File:TsavongLah-FF109.png|thumb|right|250px|Warmaster Tsavong Lah used a clawed radank leg in place of his left arm.]]
{{Quote|Our need will be great today—give him the arm.|Tsavong Lah sacrifices his arm to his gods before replacing it with a radank leg|<ref name="Star">''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]''</ref>}}
In existence as of [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]],<ref name="TERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star]]'' to [[27 ABY/Legends|27 ABY]], and the events of ''[[Legacy of the Force: Tempest]]'' to [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]].</ref> radanks were known to the sentient Yuuzhan Vong by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of their [[Yuuzhan Vong War|invasion]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. By then, some Yuuzhan Vong considered radanks as pests. In that [[Standard year/Legends|year]], as [[Gender/Legends|his]] forces [[Battle of Borleias (Yuuzhan Vong War; First)|assaulted]] the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]–allied [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Borleias/Legends|Borleias]], Yuuzhan Vong [[Warmaster]] [[Tsavong Lah]] cut off his left arm at the elbow and grafted a radank leg in its place. Lah sacrificed his arm to his [[Yun'o|gods]] to assure victory over his foes, and the left arm was chosen because Borleias was the left half of a pincer attack that Lah planned in order to capture the New Republic [[Capital/Legends|capital]] planet, [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].<ref name="Star" />

However, Lah soon encountered problems with the grafting, as the radank leg began to aggressively take over the upper part of his left arm due to [[Saboteur/Legends|sabotage]] by members of the Yuuzhan Vong [[shaper caste]].<ref name="Star" /> When Lah discovered the sabotage with the help of the shaper [[Nen Yim]],<ref name="Rebel Dream" /> the warmaster had the shapers he believed responsible put to [[Death/Legends|death]], and Yim aided the warmaster in preventing the radank claw from continuing to claim parts of his arm.<ref name="Rebel Stand" />

In [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]],<ref name="TERC" /> following the Yuuzhan Vong's defeat in the war, at least one radank existed on Coruscant, which the Yuuzhan Vong had partially [[Terraforming/Legends|terraformed]] during the invasion. The radank dwelt in the planet's [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|underworld]] and at one point was about to kill a [[Unidentified Falleen (Radank)|Falleen]] when the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Alema Rar]] used [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to [[Telekinesis/Legends|throw]] a [[Unidentified younger brother|young Human]] at the beast after he and his [[Unidentified older brother|brother]] offended her. This prompted the older brother to rush to his sibling's aid, and a fight ensued between them and the beast.<ref name="Tempest" />

==Behind the scenes==
The radank species first appeared in the form of Warmaster Tsavong Lah's claw-grafted arm in [[The New Jedi Order: Star by Star|''Star by Star'']], a [[2001]] novel written by [[Troy Denning]] as a part of [[Star Wars: The New Jedi Order|''The New Jedi Order'']] series.<ref name="Star" /> The creature itself first appeared in ''[[Legacy of the Force: Tempest]]'', another novel by Denning that was published in [[2006]] as part of the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy of the Force]]'' series.<ref name="Tempest" />
